title: malpighia lancifolia
intro: Malpighia lancifolia, also known as the 'Wild Crape Myrtle', is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and providing relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, Malpighia lancifolia is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
